commitMessage:
Bug 551496 - [14] Remove JCP disclaimers from code, UI and marketplace

Change-Id: I83d9bb822140edf0e641eb61f759fb1ee7e502f2
Signed-off-by: Jay Arthanareeswaran <jarthana@in.ibm.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit removes legal disclaimers/comments from various source files. It is an administrative/licensing change, not a code transformation that alters the logic or structure of Java code and thus cannot be generalized into a DSL rule.
summary: This commit removes specific JCP disclaimers from multi-line comments in various Java source files. This is a metadata/licensing change and does not represent a generalizable code transformation pattern. Therefore, it is not applicable for DSL rule mining.

summary: This commit removes JCP disclaimers from source file comments and updates Javadoc `@since` tags. Similar to the previous commit, these are metadata and documentation changes, not actual code transformations. Therefore, it is not applicable for DSL rule mining.

commitMessage:

Signed-off-by: Jay Arthanareeswaran <jarthana@in.ibm.com>
irrelevantReason: Changes are confined to Javadoc `@since` tags, indicating a version update for API documentation, not a code transformation or refactoring pattern.
summary: This commit updates `@since` Javadoc tags from '3.21' to '3.22' across several JDT DOM classes. This is a documentation metadata change, not a transformation of executable code. Therefore, it is not relevant for generating a TriggerPattern DSL rule.

commitMessage:
Bug 561193 - [14]record keyword inside method not colored correctly

Change-Id: Icd6ca112cab3506f7835f14d99fa17eb33eb0277
Signed-off-by: Kalyan Prasad Tatavarthi <kalyan_prasad@in.ibm.com>
irrelevantReason: This commit modifies the internal Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) conversion logic within Eclipse JDT Core to correctly handle 'record' declarations when they appear inside methods. This is a bug fix for the JDT's own parsing and internal representation of Java language constructs (specifically records introduced in Java 16), rather than a code transformation pattern applicable to user-written Java code. The TriggerPattern DSL is designed for transforming user code, not for modifying the internal behavior of the JDT parser itself.
summary: This commit is an internal JDT bug fix related to parsing and representing Java 'record' declarations in the AST, not a user-facing code transformation pattern.
